# NeighborhoodOS

> An open-source operating system for neighborhoods that want to solve their own problems. Pick one sharp pain, ship a working tool in weeks, rotate to the next pain when it is handled well.

## What it is
NeighborhoodOS is a civic-tech platform piloting in West Waldo, Kansas City, Missouri. It is open-source and node-based - any neighborhood can stand up its own instance.

## How it works
1. Pick one specific, costly, recurring pain on the block.
2. Build a small tool around just that pain.
3. Use the tool to strengthen trust, local capacity, and self-governance.
4. When that pain is handled well, rotate to the next one.

The pain has to be: concrete, costly, recurring, and actionable by the neighborhood itself.

## Current pilot
West Waldo, Kansas City - home-maintenance coordination (see neighborhoodos-homecare).

## Who runs it
Maintained by Simon L. Paige and the West Waldo neighborhood. Open contribution welcome.

## Important pages
- [Home](https://neighborhoodos.org/): platform overview
- [Launch playbook](https://neighborhoodos.org/launch): 30-day steps to start a node in your own neighborhood

## Contact
- Repo: github.com/SimonPaige/neighborhoodos (link from site)
- Email: simon@simonlpaige.com (project lead)
